title: Grandfather hears with his hands
summary: In Vienna, a young woman visits her retired grandfather, a piano tuner, and asks him how he still hears the notes.

body.speaker: Elena
body.text: Grandpa, are you tuning the piano again? You said last month you stopped.

body.speaker: Otto
body.text: I stopped working. I did not stop listening. There is a difference.

body.speaker: Elena
body.text: But your ears, Grandpa. Mama said your hearing is not so good now.

body.speaker: Otto
body.text: She is right. My ears are old. But the piano is not only ears, my dear. Sit. Put your hand here on the wood.

body.speaker: Elena
body.text: On the side of the piano?

body.speaker: Otto
body.text: Yes. Now I press one key. Feel it?

body.speaker: Otto
body.text: That is the note. The wood is saying the note to your hand. When my ears were young, I heard with my ears. Now I hear with my hands. The same note, the same wood, the same answer.

body.speaker: Elena
body.text: Can you really feel if a note is wrong?

body.speaker: Otto
body.text: I can. A wrong note shakes the wood differently. The wood is honest. The wood does not pretend.

body.speaker: Elena
body.text: How long have you tuned pianos?

body.speaker: Otto
body.text: Fifty-one years. I tuned the piano in the small concert hall on Argentinierstrasse. I tuned old pianos in old apartments. I tuned a piano for a child once who later became famous. He still sends me a card every Christmas.

body.speaker: Elena
body.text: Will you teach me to tune?

body.speaker: Otto
body.text: No, Elena. I will teach you to listen. The tuning comes later. The listening is the long part.

annotations.meaningVi: thính giác / khả năng nghe (danh từ)
rung / lắc (ngôi thứ ba số ít)

grammarNotes.title: will + verb (offers, promises, future facts)
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Cấu trúc 'will + V (nguyên thể)' dùng để hứa, đề nghị, hoặc nói về tương lai chắc chắn. Trong bài: 'Will you teach me to tune?' (đề nghị/hỏi tương lai) và 'I will teach you to listen.' (lời hứa). Không dùng to-V sau will. Sai: I will to teach. Đúng: I will teach.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: The structure 'will + base verb' is used for promises, offers, and certain future statements. In the dialogue: 'Will you teach me to tune?' (question about the future) and 'I will teach you to listen.' (a promise). Do not put 'to' after will. Wrong: I will to teach. Right: I will teach.

translationVi:
Elena: Ông ơi, ông lại đang lên dây đàn à? Tháng trước ông bảo ông nghỉ rồi mà.
Otto: Ông nghỉ làm. Ông không nghỉ nghe. Hai chuyện khác nhau.
Elena: Nhưng tai ông, ông ạ. Mẹ con bảo tai ông giờ kém rồi.
Otto: Mẹ con đúng. Tai ông già rồi. Nhưng đàn piano không chỉ là tai, cháu yêu. Ngồi xuống. Đặt tay lên gỗ này.
Elena: Lên cạnh đàn ạ?
Otto: Phải. Bây giờ ông nhấn một phím. Cháu cảm thấy gì không?
Elena: Có. Nó hơi rung.
Otto: Đó là nốt. Gỗ đang 'nói' nốt đó vào tay cháu. Hồi tai ông còn trẻ, ông nghe bằng tai. Bây giờ ông nghe bằng tay. Vẫn nốt đó, vẫn gỗ đó, vẫn câu trả lời đó.
Elena: Ông thật sự cảm được nốt nào sai sao?
Otto: Được chứ. Nốt sai làm gỗ rung khác đi. Gỗ thật thà. Gỗ không giả vờ.
Elena: Ông lên dây đàn bao lâu rồi?
Otto: Năm mươi mốt năm. Ông lên dây cho phòng hoà nhạc nhỏ ở Argentinierstrasse. Ông lên dây cho đàn cũ trong các căn hộ cũ. Có lần ông lên dây cho một đứa trẻ, sau này nó nổi tiếng. Nó vẫn gửi ông thiệp mỗi Giáng Sinh.
Elena: Ông dạy cháu lên dây nhé?
Otto: Không, Elena. Ông sẽ dạy cháu nghe. Lên dây để sau. Việc nghe mới là phần dài.

questions.prompt: What was Otto's job before he retired?
questions.explanation: He talks about tuning pianos for fifty-one years.
questions.hint: Read his long line near the end.

questions.prompt: Why was Elena surprised at the beginning?
options: She thought her grandfather had stopped
She thought he could not walk
She thought the piano was sold

questions.prompt: What distinction does Otto make at the start?
options: He stopped working but did not stop listening
He stopped tuning but kept playing
He stopped at home but works elsewhere
He stopped today but will start tomorrow
questions.explanation: 'I stopped working. I did not stop listening.'

questions.prompt: Where does he ask Elena to put her hand?
options: On the wood of the piano
questions.explanation: 'Put your hand here on the wood.'

questions.prompt: Why does he say the wood is 'honest'?
options: Because it shakes differently for a wrong note and does not pretend
Because it is from a famous tree
questions.explanation: 'The wood is honest. The wood does not pretend.'

questions.prompt: For how many years has Otto tuned pianos?
questions.hint: His answer to the duration question.

questions.prompt: Who still sends Otto a card every Christmas?
options: A child he tuned for, now famous
questions.explanation: 'He still sends me a card every Christmas.'

questions.prompt: What will Otto teach Elena instead of tuning?
questions.explanation: 'I will teach you to listen. The tuning comes later.'

options: Real skill begins with attention, not with the visible tool.
Old pianos are better than new ones.
questions.explanation: Otto's last line frames listening as the long, deep part.

cloze.template:

Otto: I stopped working. I did not stop {{b2}}. There is a difference.
Elena: But your ears, Grandpa. Mama said your hearing is not so good now.
Otto: She is right. My ears are old. But the piano is not only ears, my dear. Sit. Put your hand here on the {{b3}}.
Elena: On the side of the piano?
Otto: Yes. Now I press one key. {{b4}} it?
Elena: Yes. It moves a little.
Otto: That is the {{b5}}. The wood is saying the note to your hand. When my ears were young, I heard with my ears. Now I hear with my {{b6}}. The same note, the same wood, the same answer.
Elena: Can you really feel if a note is wrong?
Otto: I can. A wrong note shakes the wood {{b7}}. The wood is honest. The wood does not pretend.
Elena: How long have you tuned pianos?
Otto: {{b8}} years. I tuned the piano in the small concert hall on Argentinierstrasse. I tuned old pianos in old apartments. I tuned a piano for a child once who later became {{b9}}. He still sends me a card every Christmas.
Elena: Will you teach me to tune?
Otto: No, Elena. I will teach you to {{b10}}. The tuning comes later. The listening is the long part.

blanks.explanation: He stopped working but not listening.
On the wood of the piano.
Now he hears with his hands.
He will teach her to listen.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Otto says, 'The listening is the long part.' Think of a skill you would like to learn. What is the slow, hidden 'listening' part of that skill — the part most people skip — and could you start there instead of the visible part?
